fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Vehicle Parking Area; Pedestrian Walkways
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access and movement could be achieved by ensuring main access routes to the property and between multiple structures remain unobstructed and clearly marked. Augmenting signage at entry points and pathways within the landscaped area may support navigation and help guide responders efficiently. Maintaining open side access routes could improve their ability to engage with all areas of the property effectively. Additionally, ensuring that vehicle parking areas are organized and do not impede primary access routes or pedestrian walkways may facilitate staging and maneuverability for first responders. Consistent maintenance of clear and direct pathways around the main building and across connecting walkways could enhance visibility and improve overall operational response effectiveness on the property.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Fence; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property by creating greater separation between vegetation and structures could improve wildfire resilience. Thinning the scattered trees to increase spacing and reduce continuous fuel layers may minimize opportunities for fire to spread. Managing and trimming vegetation along the perimeter and adjacent to fences could reduce potential fire pathways, while careful removal or relocation of vegetation touching the access route can support firefighter movement and suppression efforts. Efforts to sustain and periodically reassess defensible space and maintain these practices long-term may further bolster the property's readiness for wildfire events.
